message:
feat(core): add asupersync runtime compat shims for storage, snapshot, replay, IPC, and runtime

Introduce cfg-gated dual-runtime paths so these modules work under both
the current tokio runtime and the future asupersync runtime. When built
with `feature = "asupersync-runtime"`, channel operations receive an
explicit `cx` context parameter matching asupersync's cancellation model;
the tokio path remains unchanged.

storage.rs:
- Extract WriteCommandSender wrapper around mpsc::Sender<WriteCommand>
  that dispatches to the correct send signature based on feature flag.
- Proxy max_capacity(), capacity(), is_closed() through the wrapper.
- writer_loop(): replace blocking_recv() with cfg-gated block_on +
  async recv for asupersync, preserving existing tokio blocking path.
  This is required because asupersync channels are always async.

snapshot_engine.rs:
- Interval-based scheduler: cfg-gate shutdown.changed() calls to pass
  context under asupersync, keeping tokio path identical.
- Intelligent scheduler: cfg-gate both trigger_rx.recv() and
  shutdown.changed() in the select! arms with async wrapper blocks
  that adapt the calling convention per runtime.

replay.rs:
- Player::Pause loop: cfg-gate control_rx.changed() to pass context.
- check_control(): cfg-gate has_changed()/borrow_and_update() vs
  has_changed()/borrow_and_clone() since asupersync watch channels
  have different borrowing semantics (no mutable borrow_and_update).

ipc.rs:
- IPC server accept loop: cfg-gate shutdown_rx.recv() to pass context
  in the select! shutdown arm.

runtime.rs:
- Wrap config_tx (watch::Sender<HotReloadableConfig>) in Arc to make
  it Clone-compatible with asupersync's watch::Sender which does not
  implement Clone. This affects ObservationRuntime and RuntimeHandle.
  The Arc adds negligible overhead since config_tx is rarely cloned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
